content_en: He also worked on translating and commenting on the Quran. Al-Sindi, Muhammad Hayaat ['-i.ll.l •t.,;... .i-..]: Muhammad Hayaat al-Sindi originally came from an area around Hyderabad, India. He moved and lived in Makkah. He spent much of his time studying hadith. One of his students whom he greatly influenced to study hadith was Muhammad ibn Abdul Wahhaab, the leader of the Muslim movement in the Najd region. Al-Sindi wrote a small commentary to al-Nawawi's Forth Hadith. Al-Subki, Taqi al-Deen [µ.I l.J:!.ll ׇ]: Taqi al-Deen Ali ibn Abdul Kaafi al-Subki (683/1284-756/1355) was born in Subk, Egypt. He studied in Cairo and became, undoubtedly, the leading Shafi'ee scholar of his time. He wrote a large number of works related mostly to legal theory and fiqh. He is the first one who attempted to complete al-Nawawi's Majmoo but he died before completing that work. In 739, he moved to Damascus and worked for years in the judiciary. His son, Taaj al-Deen al-Subki, was also a Shafi'ee scholar of note. Sufyaan ibn Abdullah [..ill ¥- U; u4i-w] - see p. 825, Sufyaan al-Thauri ['-i.J.;11 u4i-w]: Abu Abdullah Sufyaan ibn Masrooq al-Thauri (97 /716-161/778) was called the "Leader of the Believers" in Hadith. He was born and brought up in Kufah. He refused to accept the post of judge offered by Mansoor. He left Kufah in 144/761 and lived in Makkah and Madinah. Among his books are two collections of hadith, one large and one small. He studied under many of the Followers who had taken their knowledge from the Companions of the Prophet (peace be upon him). He himself had numerous important students, including Abu Hanifah, Malik, al-Auzaai and Yahya ibn Saeed al-Qattaan. In addition to being a great scholar of hadith, he was also recognized for his knowledge of ta/seer. Sufyaan ibn Uyainah [ࡨ U; u4i-w]: Abu Muhammad Sufyan ibn Uyainah ibn Maimoon (107/725-198/814) was born in Kufah but settled in Makkah where he died. A well known transmitter of hadith, he left a collection of hadith, al-Jaami. Sultaan, Naadhim [ul..hl.. ƚw]: Naadhim Sultaan is a contemporary author who lives in Kuwait. He has been the president of the Daar al-Turaath al-Islaami (Daar of Islamic Heritage). He wrote a useful commentary on the Forty Hadith of al­ Nawawi. He has also written a number of smaller books. Al-Suyooti, Jalaal al-Deen [i}-ࡩI l.J:!.ll J'.4]: Al-Suyooti (849/1 445-9 1 111505) was one of the most prolific writers in Muslim history. He was a student of ibn Hajr. His work on the sciences of hadith, Tadreeb al-Raawi (which is a Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi commentary on al-Nawawi's Taqreeb) is one of the major references for that field. He attempted to collect together all of the known hadith of the Prophet (peace be upon him) in a work known as al-Jaami al-Kabeer. He made another collection of hadith entitled al-Jaami al-Sagheer. He also compiled a ta/seer entitled al-Durr al-Manthoor. Taawoos [l.>"JJL.b]: Taawoos was a scholar from the time of the Followers. He narrated hadith from the "four Abdullabs". It is said that he met with fifty of the Companions of the Prophet (peace be upon him). But he spent a great deal of time with ibn Abbas and he learned much of his knowledge of ta/seer directly from ibn Abbas. It is narrated that ibn Abbas said about him, "I suspect that Taawoos is one of the inhabitants of Paradise." He died in the year 106 A.H. Al-Tabaraani [_,.;lǆI]: Abu al-Qaasim Sulaimaan ibn Ahmad al-Lakhami al-Tabaraani (260/873-360/97 1 ) was born in Palestine. He traveled for many years seeking knowledge and learning hadith from its masters. He literally bad hundreds, probably well over a thousand, of teachers who he learned from. After traveling and learning, he lived in Isfahaan, where he taught hadith for over sixty years. One of his collections of hadith, al-Mujam al-Kabeer, remains as one of the largest collections ofhadith aseembled. Al-Tabari, Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Jareer [i.;ǆ1 ..>.l..» L>! ª ÷ Y.l]: Al-Tabari (224/839-3 1 0/923) was a great historian, an eminent jurist of the rank of mujtahid, and an outstanding commentator on the Quran. He was born in Tabaristan and settled in Baghdad where he died. His Quranic commentary, Jaami' al-Bayan fl Taweel Ayi al-Quran, is considered by many to be the greatest commentary available that is based on the tradition of the early generations. No less outstanding is his work of history, Tareekh al-Umum wa­ l-Mulook. He is also the author of Tahdheeb al-Athaar, Ikhtilaaf al-Fuqahaa and Adab al-Qudaat. Al-Tahaawi, Abu Jafar [i.,.>J࡮I ÷ Y.l]: Abu Jafar Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Tahaawi (239-321 ) was born in Taha, a town in Upper Egypt. His father was a scholar of hadith and literature. His mother too was learned. Both of his parents used to attend the lectures of Imam al-Shafi'ee. Abu Ja'ar received his first lessons in hadith from his father. After that, he pursued the study of hadith from his maternal uncle, al-Muzani (d. 264/877), the leading student of Shafi'ee.
content_id:
Muhammad Hayat al-Sindi: Beliau berasal dari daerah sekitar Hyderabad, India, kemudian pindah dan menetap di Makkah. Beliau menghabiskan banyak waktunya untuk mempelajari hadis. Salah satu muridnya yang sangat beliau pengaruhi untuk menekuni ilmu hadis adalah Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab, pemimpin gerakan Islam di wilayah Najd. Al-Sindi menulis syarah ringkas atas Arba'in al-Nawawi.

Taqi al-Din Ali ibn Abd al-Kafi al-Subki (683/1284-756/1355): Beliau lahir di Subk, Mesir, belajar di Kairo, dan tidak diragukan lagi menjadi ulama Syafi'i terkemuka di zamannya. Beliau menulis sejumlah besar karya yang berkaitan dengan teori hukum dan fiqih. Beliaulah yang pertama kali mencoba melengkapi al-Majmu' karya al-Nawawi, namun beliau wafat sebelum menyelesaikannya. Pada tahun 739 H, beliau pindah ke Damaskus dan bekerja di bidang peradilan selama bertahun-tahun. Putranya, Taj al-Din al-Subki, juga merupakan ulama Syafi'i yang terkemuka.

Abu Abdullah Sufyan ibn Masruq al-Tsauri (97/716-161/778): Beliau dijuluki 'Amir al-Mu'minin fil Hadits' (Pemimpin Orang-orang Beriman dalam Hadis). Beliau lahir dan dibesarkan di Kufah. Beliau menolak tawaran jabatan hakim dari Khalifah Mansur. Beliau meninggalkan Kufah pada tahun 144/761 dan tinggal di Makkah dan Madinah. Di antara karya-karyanya terdapat dua kumpulan hadis, satu yang besar dan satu yang kecil. Beliau belajar dari banyak Tabi'in yang mengambil ilmunya dari para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ. Beliau sendiri memiliki banyak murid penting, di antaranya Abu Hanifah, Malik, al-Auza'i, dan Yahya ibn Said al-Qattan. Selain dikenal sebagai ulama hadis yang agung, beliau juga diakui atas penguasaannya dalam ilmu tafsir.

Abu Muhammad Sufyan ibn Uyainah ibn Maimun (107/725-198/814): Beliau lahir di Kufah namun kemudian menetap di Makkah hingga wafat. Beliau adalah perawi hadis yang terkenal dan meninggalkan sebuah kumpulan hadis yang dikenal dengan nama al-Jami'.

Nadhim Sultan: Beliau adalah penulis kontemporer yang tinggal di Kuwait dan pernah menjabat sebagai ketua Dar al-Turats al-Islami (Pusat Warisan Islam). Beliau menulis syarah yang bermanfaat atas Arba'in al-Nawawi karya Imam al-Nawawi, serta sejumlah kitab-kitab kecil lainnya.

Jalal al-Din al-Suyuti (849/1445-911/1505): Beliau adalah salah satu penulis paling produktif dalam sejarah Islam. Beliau adalah murid Ibn Hajar. Karyanya dalam ilmu hadis, Tadrib al-Rawi (yang merupakan syarah atas Taqrib al-Nawawi), menjadi salah satu rujukan utama dalam bidang tersebut. Beliau berupaya mengumpulkan seluruh hadis Nabi ﷺ yang diketahui dalam sebuah karya berjudul al-Jami' al-Kabir. Beliau juga menyusun kumpulan hadis lain berjudul al-Jami' al-Shaghir, serta menyusun tafsir yang dikenal dengan al-Durr al-Manthur.

Tawus: Beliau adalah ulama dari generasi Tabi'in yang meriwayatkan hadis dari 'empat Abdullah'. Dikatakan bahwa beliau bertemu dengan lima puluh Sahabat Nabi ﷺ. Namun beliau menghabiskan banyak waktu bersama Ibn Abbas dan mengambil banyak ilmu tafsir langsung darinya. Diriwayatkan bahwa Ibn Abbas berkata tentangnya: 'Aku menduga bahwa Tawus termasuk penghuni surga.' Beliau wafat pada tahun 106 H.

Abu al-Qasim Sulaiman ibn Ahmad al-Lakhami al-Tabarani (260/873-360/971): Beliau lahir di Palestina. Beliau mengembara selama bertahun-tahun untuk menuntut ilmu dan mempelajari hadis dari para pakarnya. Beliau memiliki ratusan guru, bahkan mungkin lebih dari seribu orang. Setelah mengembara dan belajar, beliau menetap di Isfahan di mana beliau mengajarkan hadis selama lebih dari enam puluh tahun. Salah satu kumpulan hadisnya, al-Mu'jam al-Kabir, tetap menjadi salah satu kumpulan hadis terbesar yang pernah disusun.

Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari (224/839-310/923): Beliau adalah sejarawan agung, ahli hukum terkemuka setingkat mujtahid, dan komentator Al-Quran yang luar biasa. Beliau lahir di Tabaristan dan menetap di Baghdad hingga wafat. Tafsir Al-Qurannya, Jami' al-Bayan fi Ta'wil Ayi al-Quran, dianggap oleh banyak pihak sebagai tafsir terbaik yang berdasarkan tradisi generasi awal Islam. Tidak kalah pentingnya adalah karya sejarahnya, Tarikh al-Umam wal-Muluk. Beliau juga adalah penulis Tahdzib al-Atsar, Ikhtilaf al-Fuqaha, dan Adab al-Qudah.

Abu Ja'far Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Tahawi (239-321 H): Beliau lahir di Taha, sebuah kota di Mesir Atas. Ayahnya adalah seorang ulama hadis dan sastra, demikian pula ibunya yang juga seorang yang berilmu. Kedua orang tuanya dahulu menghadiri ceramah-ceramah Imam al-Syafi'i. Abu Ja'far menerima pelajaran pertamanya dalam hadis dari ayahnya. Setelah itu, beliau melanjutkan studi hadis dari pamannya dari pihak ibu, al-Muzani (w. 264/877), murid utama al-Syafi'i.
content_ar: He أيضاً worked on translating and commenting on the القرآن الكريم. Al-Sindi, محمد ﷺ Hayaat ['-i.ll.l •t.,;... .i-..]: محمد ﷺ Hayaat al-Sindi originally came from an area around Hyderabad, India. He moved and lived in مكة المكرمة. He spent much of his time studying الحديث. One of his students whom he greatly influenced to study الحديث was محمد ﷺ ibn Abdul Wahhaab, the leader of the مسلم movement in the Najd region. Al-Sindi wrote a small commentary to النووي's Forth الحديث. Al-Subki, Taqi al-Deen [µ.I l.J:!.ll ׇ]: Taqi al-Deen علي ibn Abdul Kaafi al-Subki (683/1284-756/1355) was born in Subk, Egypt. He studied in Cairo and became, undoubtedly, the leading Shafi'ee scholar of his time. كتب a large number of works related mostly to legal theory and الفقه. He is the أولاً one who attempted to complete النووي's Majmoo but he died قبل completing that work. In 739, he moved to Damascus and worked for years in the judiciary. His son, Taaj al-Deen al-Subki, was أيضاً a Shafi'ee scholar of note. Sufyaan ibn Abdullah [..ill ¥- U; u4i-w] - see p. 825, Sufyaan al-Thauri ['-i.J.;11 u4i-w]: Abu Abdullah Sufyaan ibn Masrooq al-Thauri (97 /716-161/778) was called the "Leader of the المؤمنون" in الحديث. He was born and brought up in Kufah. He refused to accept the post of judge offered by Mansoor. He left Kufah in 144/761 and lived in مكة المكرمة and المدينة المنورة. Among his الكتب are two collections of الحديث, one large and one small. He studied under many of the Followers who had taken their العلم from the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). He himself had numerous المهم students, بما في ذلك Abu Hanifah, مالك, al-Auzaai and Yahya ibn Saeed al-Qattaan. بالإضافة إلى being a great scholar of الحديث, he was أيضاً recognized for his العلم of ta/seer. Sufyaan ibn Uyainah [ࡨ U; u4i-w]: Abu محمد ﷺ Sufyan ibn Uyainah ibn Maimoon (107/725-198/814) was born in Kufah but settled in مكة المكرمة where he died. A well known transmitter of الحديث, he left a collection of الحديث, al-Jaami. Sultaan, Naadhim [ul..hl.. ƚw]: Naadhim Sultaan is a contemporary author who lives in Kuwait. He has been the president of the Daar al-Turaath al-Islaami (Daar of Islamic Heritage). كتب a useful commentary on the Forty الحديث of al­ Nawawi. He has أيضاً written a number of smaller الكتب. Al-Suyooti, Jalaal al-Deen [i}-ࡩI l.J:!.ll J'.4]: Al-Suyooti (849/1 445-9 1 111505) was one of the most prolific writers in مسلم history. He was a student of ibn Hajr. His work on the sciences of الحديث, Tadreeb al-Raawi (which is a Commentary on the Forty الحديث of النووي commentary on النووي's Taqreeb) is one of the major references for that field. He attempted to collect together all of the known الحديث of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) in a work known as al-Jaami al-Kabeer. He made another collection of الحديث entitled al-Jaami al-Sagheer.
